cases.title: [Languages] Languages page displays alert banner, required languages card, and language list
cases.preconditions: User is on the Languages page (navigate via app header Languages tab)

steps.content: Observe the Languages page layout
steps.expected: Page shows an info alert banner containing a 'Learn more' link, and a 'Your required languages' card with a language list below it

steps.content: Observe the 'Add language' button in the top right of the card
steps.expected: Button is visible — shown with a premium icon and disabled on Free plan; shown as enabled '+ Add Language' on paid plans

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed Account Owner/Admin/Editor role variants and Webapp Free/Agency/Trial plan variants — identical steps. Dropped static title text and alert banner copy verification steps. Wix source C16431 included in source IDs (same content). Merged display-of-page-level assertions into two behavioral steps.

cases.title: [Languages] 'Learn more' link in the info alert opens multilingual banner documentation
cases.preconditions: User is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click the 'Learn more' link in the info alert banner
steps.expected: 'How to Add a Multilingual Cookie Consent Banner?' documentation page opens

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role and plan variants. Dropped navigation steps (moved to preconditions). Dropped static text verification of the alert banner content — only the click-and-navigate behavior is tested.

cases.title: [Languages] Geo-target nudge popup appears after adding a second language on a paid plan
cases.preconditions: Account is on a paid plan (Basic or higher); language list currently has exactly one language; user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click '+ Add Language', select a language from the dropdown, and click Add
steps.expected: Popup closes; the new language appears in the list; a geo-target in-app nudge popup appears

steps.content: Inspect the geo-target nudge popup
steps.expected: Popup shows a feature image, title 'Geo-target your banner for specific countries', body copy mentioning 'Available in: Pro and Ultimate plans', a 'help guide' link, and two buttons: 'Get geo-targeting now' and 'Dismiss'

steps.content: Click 'Get geo-targeting now'
steps.expected: In-app nudge upgrade page is displayed

cases.rewrite_notes: Dropped navigation and tab-click steps (moved to preconditions). Collapsed body text static verification into a single inspection step. Wix C16436 same content.

cases.title: [Languages] Dismissing the geo-target nudge closes the popup
cases.preconditions: Geo-target nudge popup is visible (add a second language on a paid plan to trigger it)

steps.content: Click 'Dismiss' on the geo-target nudge popup
steps.expected: Popup closes and does not reappear in the current session

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed to one step; the precondition carries the setup. Session-persistence assertion kept from C9954.

cases.title: [Languages] '+ Add Language' button opens the Add Language popup on paid plans
cases.preconditions: Account is on a paid plan (Basic or higher); user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click '+ Add Language' in the top right of the language list
steps.expected: Add Language popup opens showing title 'Add Language', 'Select language(s) *' label, a language dropdown, and Cancel and Add buttons

cases.rewrite_notes: Merged C505 (button click) and C508 (popup display) — C505 is a strict subset of C508. Collapsed role/plan variants. Dropped static title text verification inside popup.

cases.title: [Languages] Language dropdown in Add Language popup lists all available languages with search and checkboxes
cases.preconditions: Add Language popup is open (paid plan)

steps.content: Observe the language dropdown in the popup
steps.expected: Dropdown shows a search text field with 'Search...' placeholder and a scrollable list of languages with checkboxes, starting alphabetically (Abkhazian, Afar, Afrikaans, ...)

steps.content: Select a language via its checkbox, then observe the list and the selection field
steps.expected: The selected language is shown as checked and its name appears in the selection field above the search box; the already-added default language is checked and disabled. List items remain in alphabetical order (selected items are not pinned to the top of the list).

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Dropped static list enumeration of language names.

cases.title: [Languages] Dropdown selector collapses the language list; search field filters languages by text
cases.preconditions: Add Language popup is open

steps.content: Click the dropdown selector button in the top right corner of the language dropdown
steps.expected: Language list collapses

steps.content: Type a partial language name in the search text field
steps.expected: Language list filters to show only matching languages, listed alphabetically

steps.content: Scroll through the filtered list
steps.expected: User can scroll the list

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Three steps cover the two interactive elements of this dropdown.

cases.title: [Languages] Adding a language without available translations shows a note and adds it to the list
cases.preconditions: Add Language popup is open

steps.content: Select a language that has no available translation (e.g., Abkhazian) from the dropdown
steps.expected: Language is selected; a 'Translations not available' label appears inline next to it; a note reads 'Note: Translations are not available for some of the languages you have added, so the banner content that has not been translated will be displayed in English.'

steps.content: Click Add
steps.expected: Popup closes; the language appears in the language list on the Languages page

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Kept note-banner assertion as it's a non-obvious behavioral difference from the translation-available flow.

cases.title: [Languages] Adding a language with translations closes the popup and adds it to the list
cases.preconditions: Account is on a paid plan (Basic or higher); Add Language popup is open

steps.content: Select a language that has an available translation (e.g., French) from the dropdown
steps.expected: Language is selected and displayed above the dropdown

steps.content: Click Add
steps.expected: Popup closes; the selected language appears in the language list on the Languages page

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. This is the canonical happy path for the Languages feature — smoke. Dropped navigation steps.

cases.title: [Languages] Add button with no new language selected closes the popup without changes
cases.preconditions: Add Language popup is open; no language has been selected

steps.content: Open the Add Language popup without selecting any new language and click Add
steps.expected: The Add button is enabled (the existing default language is pre-selected and locked). Clicking Add with no new language selected closes the popup without adding any language to the list.

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Single-step validation case.

cases.title: [Languages] Cancel button on the Add Language popup closes it without saving
cases.preconditions: Add Language popup is open

steps.content: Click Cancel
steps.expected: Popup closes; no language is added to the language list

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Wix has two Cancel-related cases (C16448 title-specific, C16453 generic) — both collapse here.

cases.title: [Languages] Add Language on Free plan opens an upgrade nudge
cases.preconditions: Account is on the Free plan; user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Observe the 'Add Language' button in the 'Your required languages' card
steps.expected: Button is enabled and shows a premium icon

steps.content: Click the 'Add Language' button
steps.expected: An upgrade nudge popup appears with the heading 'Show banner in multiple languages as per your visitor’s preference!', the text 'Available in: All premium plans', and a 'Try Pro for free' CTA button

cases.rewrite_notes: Plan-specific case (Free plan only). GRILL: live QA2 behavior diverges from Suite 6 source C9925 — the button is NOT disabled and there is no hover tooltip. Instead the 'Add Language' button is enabled and clicking it opens an upgrade nudge ('Show banner in multiple languages...', 'Available in: All premium plans', 'Try Pro for free'). Steps and title rewritten to match observed behavior.

cases.title: [Languages] Language list shows each language's code, Default badge, and action buttons
cases.preconditions: User is on the Languages page; at least English is in the language list

steps.content: Observe the language list
steps.expected: List shows 'Language list' and 'Language code' columns; English is listed with code 'en'

steps.content: Observe the default language row
steps.expected: Default language has a 'Default' badge on the right side of its language code

steps.content: Observe the buttons on the right side of each language row
steps.expected: Each row has an 'Edit Content' button and a 3-dot menu button

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Dropped static column-name and button-label enumeration steps — kept structural assertions about what's visible.

cases.title: [Languages] 'Default' badge tooltip describes the default language fallback behavior
cases.preconditions: User is on the Languages page; at least one language has a 'Default' badge

steps.content: Hover over the 'Default' badge on the default language
steps.expected: Tooltip reads: 'The default language serves as a fallback option when your site is loaded in a language that you haven’t added here.'

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Tooltip text is the full expected string from C504 (truncated in source but reconstructed from context).

cases.title: [Languages] Edit Content button navigates to the language's Edit Content page
cases.preconditions: User is on the Languages page; at least one language is in the list

steps.content: Click the 'Edit Content' button for a language in the list
steps.expected: Edit Content page for that language opens

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed role/plan variants. Single-step navigation case.

cases.title: [Languages] 3-dot menu for the only language shows 'Change default language'; completing the flow updates the default
cases.preconditions: Only one language (the default, e.g., English) is in the language list; user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click the 3-dot menu button on the only language in the list
steps.expected: Dropdown shows 'Change default language' as the only option

steps.content: Click 'Change default language'
steps.expected: Change language popup opens with a language dropdown (current default marked), Cancel and Change buttons, and a 'Select a default language *' label

steps.content: Select a different language from the dropdown and click Change
steps.expected: Popup closes; selected language becomes the new default in the language list; toast message confirms 'Your default language was updated to [language name]'

cases.rewrite_notes: Merged C507 (3-dot menu) and C9928 (Change language popup) into one end-to-end flow case — they cover sequential steps of the same user action. Dropped static popup content inspection steps; kept functional assertions.

cases.title: [Languages] 3-dot menu for a non-default language shows Set as Default and Delete; confirming Set as Default updates the default
cases.preconditions: At least two languages are in the language list; user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click the 3-dot menu button on a non-default language
steps.expected: Dropdown shows 'Set as Default' and 'Delete' options

steps.content: Click 'Set as Default'
steps.expected: Confirmation popup appears: 'Set [language] as the default?' with warning text and Cancel / 'Set as default' buttons

steps.content: Click 'Set as default' in the confirmation popup
steps.expected: Popup closes; the selected language is now the default in the language list

cases.rewrite_notes: Merged C9927 (3-dot menu options) and C9931 (confirmation popup) into one end-to-end flow. Dropped static popup content verification. Trial-with-card equivalents C12710, C12714 included in source IDs.

cases.title: [Languages] Non-default languages show Inactive status and disabled Edit Content after plan downgrade to Free
cases.preconditions: Account was on a paid plan with multiple languages added; plan has been downgraded to Free

steps.content: Navigate to the Languages page
steps.expected: Alert banner reads 'Multi-lingual banners are not available on our free plan'; non-default languages show an 'Inactive' badge; Edit Content button is disabled for Inactive languages

steps.content: Click the 3-dot menu for an Inactive language
steps.expected: Menu shows 'Set as default' option

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed AO and Admin variants (C12715/C12716). Precondition requires a plan downgrade — this cannot be easily automated without account manipulation. Step 1 navigation is kept as a step (not precondition) because the assertion on the page is the primary test action.

cases.title: [Languages] Setting an Inactive language as default via the 3-dot menu reactivates it
cases.preconditions: Account is on Free plan after a paid-plan downgrade; multiple languages are present; at least one non-default language shows 'Inactive' status; user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click the 3-dot menu for an Inactive language and click 'Set as default'
steps.expected: Language change confirmation popup appears

steps.content: Click 'Set as default' in the popup
steps.expected: Popup closes; the language becomes the new default; its Inactive badge is removed

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed AO/Admin variants. Precondition shares state with Case 18.

cases.title: [Languages] Delete language popup confirms deletion and removes the language from the list
cases.preconditions: At least two languages are in the language list (one non-default); user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click the 3-dot menu for a non-default language and click 'Delete'
steps.expected: 'Delete language?' popup appears with warning text 'The [language_name] language and any translations you’ve added in this language will be permanently deleted.' and Cancel / 'Delete language' buttons

steps.content: Click 'Delete language'
steps.expected: Popup closes; the language is removed from the language list

cases.rewrite_notes: Collapsed what was available — C9932 (Webapp Free) and C16498 (Wix). No equivalent in Agency/Trial sections (coverage gap in Suite 6). Kept warning text assertion as it confirms the user-facing impact of a destructive action.

cases.title: [Languages] Adding multiple languages at once from the Add Language popup
cases.preconditions: Account is on a paid plan (Basic or higher); user is on the Languages page

steps.content: Click '+ Add Language' to open the popup
steps.expected: Add Language popup opens with the language dropdown

steps.content: Tick the checkboxes for two or more languages (e.g., German and Spanish) in the dropdown
steps.expected: All selected languages are checked and shown in the selection field above the search box

steps.content: Click Add
steps.expected: Popup closes; all selected languages are added to the language list in a single action

cases.rewrite_notes: GAP found during grill: the dropdown uses checkboxes (multi-select), but no draft case covered adding more than one language in a single Add action. Verified live on QA2 (Basic) — German + Spanish added together.

cases.title: [Languages] Default language 3-dot menu options are disabled when multiple languages exist
cases.preconditions: Account is on a paid plan; the language list has two or more languages (one is the default)

steps.content: Click the 3-dot menu on the default language row
steps.expected: The menu shows 'Set as Default' and 'Delete', but both options are disabled — the default language cannot be deleted or re-set as default from its own menu

cases.rewrite_notes: GAP found during grill: Case 16 covers the single-language default menu ('Change default language') and Case 17 covers the non-default menu (Set as Default + Delete). Neither covers the default language's menu when multiple languages exist. Verified live on QA2 — both menu items are disabled for the default language.

cases.title: [Languages] Searching a non-existent language in the Add Language dropdown shows an empty list
cases.preconditions: Account is on a paid plan; Add Language popup is open

steps.content: Type a string that matches no language (e.g., 'zzzz') in the Search field
steps.expected: The language list filters down to zero items (no matching languages shown)

cases.rewrite_notes: GAP found during grill: no draft case covered the no-results search state. Verified live on QA2 — the list becomes empty; note there is no explicit 'no results' message shown.
